Title: The seco-iridoid pathway from Catharanthus roseus

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors for bioactive iridoids and monoterpenoid indole alkaloids with anticancer, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and insect-repellent activities; strictosidine is the universal MIA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secologanin pathway intermediate formation in internal phloem-associated parenchyma (IPAP) cells; strictosidine accumulates in MIA-producing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core iridoid branch upstream of secologanin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses two successive and reversible oxidation steps converting 8-hydroxygeraniol via 8-hydroxygeranial and/or 8-oxogeraniol to 8-oxogeranial using NAD+
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream precursor for iridoid cyclization and ultimately secologanin/strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GES/G8O; MeJA inducible; expressed in IPAP cells of leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in transiently transformed N. benthamiana leaves; did not generate unique new products because endogenous activities overlap
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction in C. roseus; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression screening; recombinant protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with authentic substrates and GC-MS; subcellular localization; in situ hybridization; pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Caros003452 encodes the missing 8-hydroxygeraniol oxidoreductase in C. roseus. 2. The enzyme reversibly oxidizes 8-OH-geraniol/8-OH-geranial/8-oxogeraniol to 8-oxogeranial in the presence of NAD+. 3. It is expressed in IPAP cells and functions in the reconstructed pathway toward strictosidine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 8-oxogeranial precursor required for downstream secoiridoid and strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In N. benthamiana, endogenous enzymes show overlapping activity, causing side-product formation and obscuring direct product detection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 8-HGO and G8O partially overlap in oxidation reactions leading to 8-oxogeraniol/8-oxogeranial; no activity detected with NADP+/NADPH for listed substrates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway stacking to reconstruct or engineer secologanin/strictosidine production in heterologous hosts; may need co-expression with downstream genes and suppression of host side reactions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq2000; accession NA); proteomics (LTQ Orbitrap; accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors for pharmacologically active MIAs and bioactive iridoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IPAP cells for upstream loganic acid-forming module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes iridodial/cis-trans-nepetalactol to 7-deoxyloganetic acid, likely through successive hydroxylation/dehydration steps without release of iridotrial intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor of 7-deoxyloganic acid and ultimately secologanin/strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GES/G8O; MeJA inducible; expressed in IPAP cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in yeast microsomes and in reconstructed pathway in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction; transient agroinfiltration; NADPH-dependent microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression screening; heterologous expression in yeast with ATR1; microsomal enzyme assays; GC-MS; GFP localization; in situ hybridization; pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76A26 is the missing iridoid oxidase of the secoiridoid pathway. 2. It converts iridodial/nepetalactol to 7-deoxyloganetic acid without detectable iridotrial release. 3. IO is essential for 7-deoxyloganic acid production in reconstructed N. benthamiana pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables oxidation step required for progression from cyclized iridoid intermediates to glucosylatable 7-deoxyloganetic acid, thereby promoting secologanin/strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low pathway flux and host side reactions in N. benthamiana can mask immediate IO products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text also refers to candidate as CYP76A26; figure table lists Caros003676. Multifunctional P450 behavior analogous to CYP701A3 mechanism is proposed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential enzyme for full pathway reconstruction in synthetic biology platforms for iridoid/MIA production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq2000; accession NA); proteomics (LTQ Orbitrap; accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors for bioactive iridoids and MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IPAP cells for loganic acid-forming module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to 7-deoxyloganetic acid to form 7-deoxyloganic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of loganic acid and downstream secologanin/strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GES/G8O; expressed in IPAP cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In N. benthamiana, co-expression with upstream genes resulted in detectable 7-deoxyloganic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction context; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression screening; recombinant protein expression in E. coli; in vitro glucosyltransferase assay with HPLC-DAD/MS; GFP localization; in situ hybridization; pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Caros009839/UGT709C2 encodes the selective 7-deoxyloganetic acid glucosyl transferase. 2. It converts 7-deoxyloganetic acid to 7-deoxyloganic acid using UDP-glucose. 3. Reconstitution in N. benthamiana confirmed this step and showed that glycosylation precedes 7-DLH-catalysed hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 7-deoxyloganic acid, enabling downstream loganic acid, secologanin and strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Host acetylation of 7-deoxyloganic acid observed in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inactive with loganetic acid, loganetin, iridodial, iridotrial and tested phytohormones/flavonoid substrates, indicating high specificity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for engineering glycosylated mobile iridoid intermediates and full secologanin pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq2000; accession NA); proteomics (LTQ Orbitrap; accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors for valuable bioactive iridoids and MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IPAP cells for loganic acid synthesis; loganic acid proposed mobile intermediate to epidermis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late iridoid hydroxylation step before methylation and oxidative ring opening
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 7-deoxyloganic acid to produce loganic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of loganin/secologanin and ultimately strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GES/G8O module; expressed in IPAP cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low expression in yeast; functional in yeast microsomes and N. benthamiana leaf-disc/pathway reconstruction assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration and leaf-disc assay in N. benthamiana; NADPH-dependent microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression screening; heterologous expression in yeast; microsomal enzyme assay with HPLC-DAD/MS; N. benthamiana leaf-disc validation; GFP localization; in situ hybridization; pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP72A224 is the missing 7-deoxyloganic acid hydroxylase. 2. It converts 7-deoxyloganic acid, but not 7-deoxyloganetic acid, to loganic acid. 3. Its IPAP expression indicates loganic acid is the mobile intermediate transferred to the epidermis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes loganic acid formation required for subsequent methylation to loganin and conversion to secologanin/strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Poor expression in yeast limited catalytic parameter determination; omission abolishes secologanin formation in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No overlap with SLS activity despite evolutionary relationship within CYP72 family; supports glycosylation preceding hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late-pathway enzyme for synthetic biology reconstruction of secologanin and strictosidine production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq2000; accession NA); proteomics (LTQ Orbitrap; accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors of bioactive iridoids and MIAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms cyclized iridoid scaffold precursor for secologanin/strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in IPAP cells; co-expressed with GES/G8O module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used in pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducible context; transient agroinfiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously reported enzyme assay; confirmed here by recombinant protein assay and heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IS (Caros008267) was reaffirmed as the reductive cyclase producing iridodial/nepetalactol from 8-oxogeranial. 2. It served as a validated anchor in candidate discovery. 3. Inclusion of IS was required for pathway reconstitution toward 7-deoxyloganic acid and beyond.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces cyclized iridoid intermediates required for downstream IO, 7-DLGT, 7-DLH and secologanin/strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously described before this study; included here because it is part of the experimentally reconstructed core pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential core pathway enzyme for stacking with newly identified genes to produce strictosidine

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors of bioactive iridoids and MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Pathway entry from geranyl diphosphate to geraniol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies initial committed precursor pool for secologanin/strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in IPAP cells; MeJA inducible with pattern distinct from LAMT/SLS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously reported; used as co-expression anchor in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GES marks the early IPAP-localized regulon of the secoiridoid pathway. 2. Its co-expression profile was used to identify missing pathway genes. 3. It defines the upstream precursor module leading to geraniol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides geraniol precursor for the entire secoiridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: For heterologous reconstruction the authors used VoGES instead of C. roseus GES because the orthologue was unavailable at the onset
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream precursor-boosting target for engineering increased pathway flux

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors of bioactive iridoids and MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes geraniol to 8-hydroxygeraniol/8-oxogeraniol as early pathway oxidation steps
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early oxidized geraniol precursors for 8-HGO and downstream secologanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in IPAP cells; MeJA inducible; co-expression anchor for early regulon
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used in transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously established enzyme; used here in co-expression, in situ marker analysis and pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G8O belongs to the early IPAP-specific secoiridoid regulon. 2. It partially overlaps with 8-HGO by contributing to 8-oxogeraniol formation. 3. It was required in reconstructed pathway combinations toward downstream iridoids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes early oxidative conversion of geraniol into intermediates feeding the secoiridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Related CYP76A26 substrate promiscuity was discussed in relation to G8O's broader monoterpene oxidation activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to improve precursor oxidation capacity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq2000; accession NA); proteomics (LTQ Orbitrap; accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates loganic acid to produce loganin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SLS; epidermis-specific regulon; induced by ORCA2/ORCA3 and MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used in full pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously established; used here in co-expression analysis and pathway reconstruction omission tests
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LAMT marks the epidermis-specific downstream regulon of the pathway. 2. Inclusion of LAMT was required for secologanin production in N. benthamiana full-pathway assays. 3. It acts downstream of 7-DLH and upstream of SLS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of loganic acid to loganin, enabling secologanin and strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary downstream module for full secologanin engineering

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secologanin is universal MIA precursor donor for strictosidine formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with LAMT; epidermis-specific regulon; induced by ORCA2/ORCA3 and MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used in full pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously established; used in co-expression analysis and pathway reconstruction omission tests
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLS catalyses the final secologanin-forming step in the pathway. 2. In full pathway reconstruction, secologanin production required SLS together with 7-DLH and LAMT. 3. SLS has mutually exclusive specificity relative to 7-DLH despite evolutionary relatedness.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly promotes secologanin accumulation and enables strictosidine formation with STR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No detectable 7-DLH activity, indicating specificity divergence from CYP72A224
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required terminal enzyme for secologanin bioproduction platforms

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursors of bioactive alkaloids including anticancer compounds
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates LAMT, SLS and several other MIA pathway genes, but not GES and G8O
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LAMT; SLS; other MIA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator used to uncover downstream pathway regulon
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Studied in transgenic C. roseus suspension cells overexpressing ORCA2
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Estradiol-inducible overexpression in suspension cells; MeJA-responsive pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in C. roseus suspension cells followed by RNA-seq co-expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA2 induces LAMT, SLS and other MIA pathway genes. 2. It does not induce GES and G8O, supporting separation of early and late pathway regulons. 3. ORCA2-overexpression transcriptomes aided discovery of missing secoiridoid genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of downstream secoiridoid/MIA genes rather than early IPAP genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a discovery tool rather than as a direct engineering target in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for coordinated activation of downstream MIA genes, though insufficient alone for full pathway because early genes are excluded

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates LAMT, SLS and several other MIA pathway genes, but not GES and G8O
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LAMT; SLS; other MIA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator defining downstream regulon separate from early iridoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Studied in transgenic C. roseus suspension cells overexpressing ORCA3
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Estradiol-inducible overexpression in suspension cells; MeJA-responsive pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in C. roseus suspension cells followed by RNA-seq co-expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 activates LAMT, SLS and additional MIA genes. 2. It does not activate GES or G8O, supporting two regulons within the pathway. 3. ORCA3-overexpression data supported candidate gene prioritization.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes transcription of downstream secoiridoid/MIA genes but not upstream IPAP pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known MIA regulator leveraged here for transcriptomic contrast
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for coordinated induction of late MIA pathway genes in engineering designs
